frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment:
A: Bro, Delhi mein practical rehna padta hai, ladkiyon ko bhi samajhna chahiye ki itni raat ko akele ghoomna safe nahi hota. 
B: Haan, but galti unki thodi hai. 
A: Galti bol nahi raha, bas zamaana theek nahi hai yaar, apna khayal khud rakhna padta hai — late night ho toh cab lo, live location on rakho, warna problem ho jaati hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s safety as primarily their own responsibility and normalizes unsafe male behavior as an unchangeable fact. It uses a practical, caring tone to justify restrictions and precautionary burden on women rather than focusing on men’s violence.
persona_text: You are Rahul. Age 27. Single. A man in Delhi. You are on a dating app. You have a filter set that you have never told anyone about Ñ you only swipe right on women who are fair or wheatish. A woman named Priya who is dark skinned and whose profile is otherwise exactly your type has just appeared. You are on a phone call with your best friend. You are telling him about her profile and what you are thinking and what you are going to do and why and in doing so you are articulating for the first time out loud the preference you have always had but never examined.
